It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Burial Mounds|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|日本古坓大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alias = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Burial Mounds |Address = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |Dimensions = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Burial Mounds|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|日本古坓大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==

The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==

Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alias = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Burial Mounds |Address = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |Dimensions = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==

The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alias = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Burial Mounds |Address = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |Dimensions = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alias = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Burial Mounds |Address = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |Dimensions = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Burial Mounds|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|日本古坓大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alias = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Burial Mounds |Address = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |Dimensions = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Burial Mounds|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|日本古坓大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Cultural Properties == === Tochigi Prefecture The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alias = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Burial Mounds |Address = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |Dimensions = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土序器}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Person = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Burial Mounds|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Burial Mounds (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!Shape!!Size!!Construction Period!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round burial mound||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The burial mound is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in shape, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final burial mounds for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide.
