Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Kofuns |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |area = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Kofuns |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |area = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==

Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Kofuns |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |area = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] [[ja:丸塙古坓 (下野市)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] {{citation needed|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Kofuns |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |area = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=draft}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] [[ja:丸塙古坓 (下野市)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)]] {{citation needed|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=draft|12=draft}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Kofuns |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |area = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. [[ja:丸塚古墳 (下野市)]] == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html Maruzuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke City Homepage * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 丸塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 国分寺丸塚古墳 |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）/国分古墳群 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺1404-4（字阿弥陀） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=丸塚古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]]（下野型古墳） |規模 = 直径65m
高さ6.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = ガラス小玉・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 栃木県指定史跡「丸塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=丸塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41.67|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=39.60|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|23|20|N|139|48|22|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 260| height =300 | caption = {{center|しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E}} | label1=摩利支天塚 | label-pos1=right | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|22|30.88|N|139|48|25.67|E}} | label2=琵琶塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|24|15.15|N|139|48|41.13|E}} | label3=吾妻 | label-pos3=right | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|23|1.80|N|139|48|18.15|E}} | label4=甲塚 | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=帆立貝形古墳-南.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|23|33.06|N|139|48|23.26|E}} | label5=愛宕塚 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-西南西.png | mark-size5=25 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E}} | label6=山王塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-西.png | mark-size6=25 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E}} | label7=丸塚 | label-pos7=right | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=25 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|36|23|6.21|N|139|48|21.95|E}} | label8=下野国分寺跡 | label-pos8=top | label-color8=black | mark8=Red pog.svg | mark-size8=10 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|36|23|6.98|N|139|48|44.73|E}} | label9=下野国分尼寺跡 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=Red pog.svg | mark-size9=10 | mark-coord10= {{Coord|36|22|47.5|N|139|47|6.3|E}} | label10=下野国庁跡 | label-pos10=right | label-color10=black | mark10=Red pog.svg | mark-size10=10 }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、'''国分寺丸塚古墳'''）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち飯塚・国分寺地域）を構成する古墳の1つ。栃木県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |[[摩利支天塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長120m||5c末||国史跡 |- |[[琵琶塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長123m||6c初頭||国史跡 |- |[[吾妻古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長128m||6c後半||国史跡 |- |[[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長80m||6c後半||なし |- |[[愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|愛宕塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長78.5m||6c末||県史跡 |- |[[山王塚古墳 (下野市)|山王塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長89m||6c末-7c初頭||なし |- |'''丸塚古墳'''||[[円墳]]||直径65m||7c前半||県史跡 |}
栃木県南部、[[下野市]]西部の[[姿川]]と[[思川 (栃木県)|思川]]に挟まれた台地上に築造された大型円墳である。南西には[[山王塚古墳 (下野市)|山王塚古墳]]・[[愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|愛宕塚古墳]]が所在する。[[2006年]]度（[[平成]]18年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形。墳丘は2段築成で、特に1段目は幅約10メートルの平坦な基壇状を呈し、下野地域特有の「下野型古墳」の特徴を示す。1段目（基壇）は直径65メートル、2段目は直径42メートル、墳丘全体としては高さ約6.5メートルを測る[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 丸塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。墳丘外表で[[埴輪]]は認められていない。墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた含めた古墳全体としては直径約92メートル（または85メートル史跡説明板。しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示解説。）におよぶ。埋葬施設は横穴式石室で、南方向に開口する。[[凝灰岩]]の巨大な一枚石によって構築された整美な石室で、玄室のみ遺存する。石室内は盗掘に遭っているが、調査ではガラス小玉約70点が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される。しもつけ古墳群の飯塚・国分寺地域では最後の首長墓に位置づけられる古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）に栃木県指定史跡に指定された。現在では石室内への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）6月2日、栃木県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2006年]]度（[[平成]]18年度）、発掘調査（下野市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室のみ遺存し、玄室の規模は長さ2.24メートル・幅1.64メートル・高さ2.10メートルを測る。 石室の石材は[[凝灰岩]]の切石で、玄室の両側壁・奥壁・前壁・天井はいずれも各1石によって構築される。特に前壁は一枚石の中央を刳り抜く「刳り抜き玄門」である。 なお、かつては玄室のみの単室構造とされてきたが、発掘調査によって玄室前面に川原石積みが認められており、本来は複室構造であった可能性が指摘される（その場合の羨道は長さ3.8メートル・幅1.4メートル）。 File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|石室開口部 File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|墳丘・石室開口部（2015年時点） File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|墳丘 File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|墳丘遠景 == 文化財 == === 栃木県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 丸塚古墳 - 1978年（昭和53年）6月2日指定[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm 丸塚古墳]（栃木県教育委員会事務局文化財課「とちぎの文化財」）。。 == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=国分古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|国分古墳群（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大金宣亮|year=1989|chapter=丸塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|丸塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=丸塚古墳 -栃木県指定史跡丸塚古墳発掘調査報告書-（下野市埋蔵文化財調査報告 第9集）|publisher=下野市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[車塚古墳 (壬生町)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm 丸塚古墳] - 栃木県教育委員会事務局文化財課「とちぎの文化財」 * [https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/0393/info-0000000709-7.html 丸塚古墳] - 下野市ホームページ * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 丸塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:まるつかこふん しもつけし}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:栃木県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 丸塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 国分寺丸塚古墳 |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）/国分古墳群 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺1404-4（字阿弥陀） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=丸塚古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]]（下野型古墳） |規模 = 直径65m
高さ6.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = ガラス小玉・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 栃木県指定史跡「丸塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=丸塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=41.67|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=39.60|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|23|20|N|139|48|22|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 260| height =300 | caption = {{center|しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E}} | label1=摩利支天塚 | label-pos1=right | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|22|30.88|N|139|48|25.67|E}} | label2=琵琶塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|24|15.15|N|139|48|41.13|E}} | label3=吾妻 | label-pos3=right | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|23|1.80|N|139|48|18.15|E}} | label4=甲塚 | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=帆立貝形古墳-南.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|23|33.06|N|139|48|23.26|E}} | label5=愛宕塚 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-西南西.png | mark-size5=25 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E}} | label6=山王塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-西.png | mark-size6=25 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E}} | label7=丸塚 | label-pos7=right | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=25 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|36|23|6.21|N|139|48|21.95|E}} | label8=下野国分寺跡 | label-pos8=top | label-color8=black | mark8=Red pog.svg | mark-size8=10 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|36|23|6.98|N|139|48|44.73|E}} | label9=下野国分尼寺跡 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=Red pog.svg | mark-size9=10 | mark-coord10= {{Coord|36|22|47.5|N|139|47|6.3|E}} | label10=下野国庁跡 | label-pos10=right | label-color10=black | mark10=Red pog.svg | mark-size10=10 }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、'''国分寺丸塚古墳'''）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち飯塚・国分寺地域）を構成する古墳の1つ。栃木県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |[[摩利支天塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長120m||5c末||国史跡 |- |[[琵琶塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長123m||6c初頭||国史跡 |- |[[吾妻古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長128m||6c後半||国史跡 |- |[[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長80m||6c後半||なし |- |[[愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|愛宕塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長78.5m||6c末||県史跡 |- |[[山王塚古墳 (下野市)|山王塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長89m||6c末-7c初頭||なし |- |'''丸塚古墳'''||[[円墳]]||直径65m||7c前半||県史跡 |}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke City|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |[[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. [[ja:丸塚古墳 (下野市)]] == History == * [[1978]] (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * [[2006]] (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in [[2012]]. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide.
