Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke City|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|en|Marishitenzuka Kofun|WD=Q17210244|lt=Marishitenzuka Kofun|1=Marishitenzuka Kofun}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Biwazuka Kofun|en|Biwazuka Kofun|WD=Q17230002|lt=Biwazuka Kofun|1=Biwazuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|1=Sugata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447636}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Omoi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491516}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) / Kokubun Kofuns |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qid=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke City|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}} Kokubunji 1404-4 (阿弥陀) |location = {{coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Maruzuka Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimotsuke-style kofun) |area = Diameter: 65m
Height: 6.5m |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Glass beads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須惧器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土序器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual = |Historic Site = Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun" }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塙古坓, also known as Kokubunji Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qid=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke City|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja|しもつけ古坓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) and has been designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+List of Shimotsuke Kofuns (Iizuka-Kokubunji area) !Mound name!!type!!area!!built!!Historic Site Status |- |{{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|en|Marishitenzuka Kofun|qid=Q17210244|lt=Marishitenzuka Kofun|1=Marishitenzuka Kofun}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Mound length: 120m||Late 5th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Biwazuka Kofun|en|Biwazuka Kofun|qid=Q17230002|lt=Biwazuka Kofun|1=Biwazuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 123m||Early 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|君媛古坓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128m||Late 6th century||National Historic Site |- |{{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|屈翼貝形古坓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Mound length: 80m||Late 6th century||None |- |{{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 78.5m||Late 6th century||Prefectural Historic Site |- |{{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 89m||Late 6th to early 7th century||None |- |'''Maruzuka Kofun'''||Round Kofun||Diameter: 65m||Early 7th century||Prefectural Historic Site |}
The Kofun is situated in the southern part of Tochigi Prefecture, on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|1=Sugata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447636}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|思川 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi Prefecture)|1=Omoi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11491516}} in the western area of Shimotsuke City. Nearby are the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|山王塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛孝塙古坓 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Excavations were conducted in 2006 (Heisei 18). The mound is round in type, built in two tiers. The first tier is a flat, pedestal-like foundation about 10 meters wide, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun."[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=9 Maruzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke City Virtual Cultural Assets Museum). The first tier has a diameter of 65 meters, while the second tier measures 42 meters in diameter, with the entire mound reaching a height of about 6.5 meters. The surrounding moat extends the mound's overall diameter to approximately 92 meters. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is built with large tuff stones and is noted for its fine construction. Although the chamber has been looted, about 70 glass beads were recovered during excavations.Historical site explanation board. The mound dates back to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, marking it as one of the final Kofuns for local rulers in the Shimotsuke region. == History == * {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53), designated as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] (Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|qid=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} (Heisei 18), excavation conducted by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2012|en|2012|qid=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}. == burial == [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Only the burial chamber (現室) remains, measuring 2.24 meters long, 1.64 meters wide, and 2.10 meters high. The chamber walls, rear wall, front wall, and ceiling are each constructed from single slabs of tuff stone. The front wall features a unique "carved doorway" design, where the entrance was carved out of a single stone. Excavation revealed river stones arranged in front of the chamber, suggesting the original structure included a multi-chamber configuration. The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide. File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), genshitsu.jpg|現室 (toward the rear wall) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-2-2.jpg|Mound and chamber entrance (2015) File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu-3.jpg|Mound File:Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), enkei.jpg|Distant view of the mound == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Maruzuka Kofun - Designated on June 2, 1978. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Literature used for this article)}} * Explanation Board (Shimotsuke City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofuns|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harv|Kokubun Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Okane Nobusuke|year=1989|chapter=Maruzuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坓大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Maruzuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塙古坓 (二重町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurazuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/2341043.htm Maruzuka Kofun] - The possible passageway would have been 3.8 meters long and 1.4 meters wide.
